varieties.name: Common Sage (Salvia officinalis) 🏆
varieties.origin: Mediterranean region (Southern Europe, Northern Africa), cultivated for 2000+ years
varieties.traits: Woody perennial subshrub, 60-90 cm tall, gray-green velvety leaves, lavender-blue flowers
varieties.growing_notes: humid subtropical climates climate: Performs well but prefers drier conditions. Requires excellent drainage. Can struggle with high summer humidity - plant in full sun with good air circulation. Tolerates Humid subtropical mild winters excellently. Harvest before flowering for best flavor.
varieties.availability: Widely available - Herb Cottage, Bunnings, local humid subtropical climates nurseries

varieties.name: Berggarten Sage (S. officinalis 'Berggarten')
varieties.traits: Compact bush 45-60 cm, larger broader leaves than common sage, rarely blooms
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent leaf production, dense bushy growth
varieties.growing_notes: Humid subtropical: Excellent choice. Mildew resistance valuable in humid summers. Full sun required. Stays compact and manageable. Hardy to mild humid subtropical climates winters. One of best performers for subtropical Queensland.
varieties.availability: Online nurseries, specialty herb suppliers in Queensland

varieties.name: Purple Sage (S. officinalis 'Purpurascens')
varieties.traits: 30-45 cm tall, purple-tinged leaves maturing to gray-green, lavender flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Humid subtropical: Less winter hardy than common sage (Zone 6+). Requires protection in cooler humid subtropical climates areas. Best grown as annual or in containers to move if needed. Full sun essential. Ornamental value high.

varieties.name: Tricolor Sage (S. officinalis 'Tricolor')
varieties.traits: 30-45 cm, variegated green, white, pink leaves, compact growth
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, milder flavor than common sage

varieties.name: Golden Sage (S. officinalis 'Icterina')
varieties.traits: 30-45 cm, gold and green variegated foliage, ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Good, slightly milder flavor, citrus notes in well-limed soil
varieties.growing_notes: Humid subtropical: Hardy to Zone 6, suitable for humid subtropical climates climate. Slow-growing advantage for low-maintenance gardens. Requires bright sun for best color. Tolerates subtropical conditions better than Tricolor.

varieties.name: Holt's Mammoth Sage (S. officinalis 'Holt's Mammoth')
varieties.origin: Commercial selection for large leaf production
varieties.traits: 75-90 cm tall, vigorous fast growth, extra-large leaves 10-12 cm

varieties.name: Dwarf Sage (S. officinalis 'Compacta')
varieties.traits: 20-25 cm tall, miniature compact form, small narrow leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Humid subtropical: Ideal for containers and small humid subtropical climates gardens. Excellent air circulation due to compact size reduces humidity issues. Perfect for apartment balconies. Zone 4-10 hardy. Propagated by cuttings only.

varieties.name: Pineapple Sage (Salvia elegans)
varieties.traits: 90-150 cm tall, bright green foliage, scarlet-red tubular flowers late summer-autumn
varieties.yield_notes: High, rapid growth in warm conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Humid subtropical: EXCELLENT for subtropical humid subtropical climates. Heat and humidity tolerant. Attracts hummingbirds and bees. Tender perennial - may die back in cold winters but resprouts. Frost-sensitive. Full sun to part shade. One of best sages for humid subtropical climates climate.
varieties.availability: Common in humid subtropical climates nurseries, Herb Cottage

varieties.name: Mexican Bush Sage (Salvia leucantha)
varieties.traits: 90-120 cm tall, dense shrubby growth, velvety purple and white flower spikes

varieties.name: Cleveland Sage (Salvia clevelandii)
varieties.traits: 60-150 cm, aromatic gray-green foliage, lavender-purple flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Humid subtropical: Moderate success. Prefers drier Mediterranean climate but adaptable. Requires excellent drainage. Drought-tolerant - important in Humid subtropical dry spells. Prune after flowering. May need protection from extreme humidity.

varieties.name: Mealycup Sage (Salvia farinacea)
varieties.traits: 60-120 cm, blue-violet flower spikes with powdery appearance

varieties.name: White Sage (Salvia apiana)
varieties.traits: 90-150 cm tall, silvery-white foliage, white flowers with lavender spots
varieties.growing_notes: Humid subtropical: CHALLENGING. Prefers very dry conditions - struggles with humid subtropical climates humidity. Requires perfect drainage and minimal summer water. May rot in wet winters. Best grown in large containers with controlled watering. Zone 8-10 but humidity is limiting factor. Sacred ceremonial use - harvest responsibly.

varieties.name: Clary Sage (Salvia sclarea) 🏆
varieties.origin: Mediterranean, Central Asia, cultivated since ancient times
varieties.traits: 60-120 cm, biennial or short-lived perennial, large textured leaves, showy flower spikes
varieties.yield_notes: Good, primarily grown for essential oil and ornamental value
varieties.growing_notes: Humid subtropical: Moderate success. Requires cold period (3+ months) for flowering - may not flower reliably in humid subtropical climates. More suitable for cooler Queensland regions (Toowoomba, Stanthorpe). Well-drained soil essential. Cultivar 'Turkestanica' more heat-tolerant.
varieties.availability: Herb nurseries, essential oil plant suppliers

varieties.name: Common Sage (Salvia officinalis) 🏆
varieties.traits: 60-90 cm woody perennial, gray-green leaves, lavender flowers
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates: VERY CHALLENGING. Does not tolerate tropical monsoon climate well. Hates wet feet and high humidity. If attempted: Grow in large containers with excellent drainage, bring under cover during heavy monsoons. May survive as short-lived annual. Not recommended for tropical monsoon climates lowlands. Better suited to cooler Indian hill stations.
varieties.availability: Limited - import seeds, very rare in tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Clary Sage (Salvia sclarea) 🏆
varieties.traits: Biennial herb with large leaves, showy flower spikes
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates: NOT SUITABLE. Successfully introduced to subtropical North Indian plains as winter annual crop (Kashmir valley produces small amounts). Requires cold period for flowering. Heavy monsoon rains cause rot. Not adapted to tropical monsoon climates climate. Best grown in cooler Indian regions.
varieties.availability: Not available in tropical monsoon climates; Kashmir/North India sources only

varieties.name: Pineapple Sage (Salvia elegans)
varieties.traits: 90-150 cm perennial, bright green leaves, scarlet tubular flowers
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates: BEST OPTION for tropical tropical monsoon climates. Highly heat and humidity tolerant. Native to tropical regions. Frost-sensitive (perfect for tropical monsoon climates - no frost). Morning sun with afternoon shade ideal. Requires regular moisture - suits monsoon climate. Protect from heavy downpours. Container growing recommended for drainage control. Most likely sage to succeed in tropical monsoon climates area.
varieties.availability: May be available through specialty herb suppliers; import seeds possible

varieties.name: Mexican Bush Sage (Salvia leucantha)
varieties.traits: 90-120 cm evergreen shrub, velvety purple flowers
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates: GOOD POTENTIAL. Drought-tolerant once established - handles dry periods between monsoons. Tropical to subtropical origin suits tropical monsoon climates. Requires excellent drainage during monsoons. Plant on slopes or raised beds. Full sun. May perform well in tropical monsoon climates if protected from waterlogging. Zones 8-10.
varieties.availability: Rare in tropical monsoon climates; seeds may be imported

varieties.name: Scarlet Sage (Salvia splendens)
varieties.traits: 30-90 cm, brilliant red flowers year-round in tropics
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates: EXCELLENT CHOICE. Native to tropical climate - ideally suited for tropical monsoon climates. Thrives in heat and humidity. Self-seeds prolifically. Year-round flowering in tropical conditions. Grows well in sun or partial shade. Perfect for tropical monsoon climates monsoon climate. One of the best Salvia species for tropical India.
varieties.availability: May be available in tropical monsoon climates nurseries as ornamental; seeds can be sourced

varieties.name: Mealycup Sage (Salvia farinacea)
varieties.traits: 60-120 cm, blue-violet flower spikes
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates: MODERATE SUCCESS POSSIBLE. Heat-tolerant but may become leggy with too much shade or water. Prefers full sun to partial shade. Requires good drainage - critical during monsoons. Grown as annual in most areas. May reseed. Benefits from afternoon shade in intense tropical monsoon climates heat.
varieties.availability: Import seeds; rare in Indian nurseries

varieties.name: Grape Scented Sage (Salvia melissodora)
varieties.traits: 150-180 cm tall, open shrub, light purple flowers
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates: GOOD POTENTIAL. Mexican origin suggests heat tolerance. Large size provides good harvest. Late summer to frost flowering (continuous in tropical monsoon climates). Tea use valuable. Requires space. Good drainage essential. May adapt to tropical monsoon climates conditions with proper site selection.

varieties.name: Fruit Sage (Salvia dorisiana)
varieties.traits: 90-120 cm, magenta-pink winter flowers, fruit salad aroma
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates: EXCELLENT OPTION. Tropical origin from Central America - well-suited to tropical monsoon climates climate. Tolerates semi-shade (benefits afternoon shade in tropical monsoon climates heat). Winter flowering in native habitat may mean year-round flowers in tropical monsoon climates. Highly aromatic for tea. One of best tropical Salvias for monsoon regions.
varieties.availability: Specialty herb imports; rare but suitable for tropical monsoon climates
